reasoning: The informal statement ("f holomorphic on an open set Ω, |f| constant ⇒ f constant") is literally false without connectedness: on Ω = two disjoint disks, f = 1 on one and f = -1 on the other is holomorphic with |f| = 1 but not constant. In Shakarchi's text (Chapter 1), "open set Ω" in this context implicitly refers to a region/domain (connected open set), so adding `IsPreconnected Ω` surfaces an implicit premise needed for truth — per the worked-example rule, this is faithful, not weaker. `DifferentiableOn ℂ f Ω` is the standard Mathlib encoding of "holomorphic on Ω", `∃ c : ℝ, ∀ z ∈ Ω, ‖f z‖ = c` correctly expresses "|f| is constant on Ω", and proving `f a = f b` for arbitrary `a b : Ω` is the standard pointwise encoding of "f is constant on Ω". No hypothesis has been silently strengthened or weakened beyond the implicit-premise allowance.
